waivers.rationale: Semgrep 1.170.0 requires click~=8.1.8, while the advisory is fixed only in Click 8.3.3. The allowed non-interactive scan path does not invoke click.edit.
mitigations: The waived dependency exists only in the ephemeral security-tool environment.
CI invokes one fixed Semgrep scan command with a repository-local ruleset.
The waiver expires automatically and blocks repository audit if not renewed or removed.

waivers.affected_api: authenticated stateful MCP HTTP server transports
waivers.rationale: At the 2026-07-16 review, Semgrep 1.170.0 was the latest release and required mcp==1.23.3, while this advisory was fixed only in MCP 1.27.2. RigorFoundry invokes only semgrep scan and never starts an authenticated stateful MCP HTTP server.
mitigations: MCP exists only in the ephemeral security-tool environment and is absent from runtime and published package dependencies.
CI invokes one fixed Semgrep scan command and never invokes semgrep mcp or an MCP server transport.
The waiver expires automatically within 30 days and blocks repository audit if not renewed or removed.

waivers.rationale: At the 2026-07-16 review, Semgrep 1.170.0 was the latest release and required mcp==1.23.3, while this advisory was fixed only in MCP 1.27.2. RigorFoundry invokes only semgrep scan and never enables experimental MCP server tasks.
mitigations: MCP exists only in the ephemeral security-tool environment and is absent from runtime and published package dependencies.
CI invokes one fixed Semgrep scan command and never invokes semgrep mcp or enables MCP server task handlers.
The waiver expires automatically within 30 days and blocks repository audit if not renewed or removed.

waivers.rationale: At the 2026-07-16 review, Semgrep 1.170.0 was the latest release and required mcp==1.23.3, while this advisory was fixed only in MCP 1.28.1. RigorFoundry invokes only semgrep scan and never wires the deprecated MCP WebSocket server transport into an application.
mitigations: MCP exists only in the ephemeral security-tool environment and is absent from runtime and published package dependencies.
CI invokes one fixed Semgrep scan command and never invokes semgrep mcp or the deprecated MCP WebSocket server transport.
The waiver expires automatically within 30 days and blocks repository audit if not renewed or removed.
